summary: Add React ErrorBoundary at app root
problem_statement: Unhandled JavaScript errors in React components currently crash the entire application with a white screen, providing no feedback to the user and no recovery path. A React ErrorBoundary at the app root catches these errors and renders a fallback UI, keeping the application usable and providing a path to recovery (reload, navigate away).
in_scope: Create a React ErrorBoundary component using the componentDidCatch / getDerivedStateFromError lifecycle
Design a simple, informative fallback UI that displays when an unhandled error is caught
Wrap the app root with the ErrorBoundary so all component tree errors are caught
Log caught errors to the console (or to the logger from P6-R2-19 if available)
out_of_scope: Server-side error handling — handled in P6-R2-17
Error reporting to an external service (Sentry, etc.) — not in R2 scope
Granular per-component error boundaries — only the app-root boundary is in scope
Handling errors in event handlers or async code (ErrorBoundary only catches render errors)
requirements: A React ErrorBoundary component exists and is mounted at the app root
The ErrorBoundary catches unhandled component errors and renders a fallback UI
The fallback UI provides the user with an actionable recovery path (e.g. reload button)
acceptance_criteria: A React ErrorBoundary catches and renders fallback UI for unhandled client errors
The fallback UI is informative and provides a recovery action
The ErrorBoundary wraps the entire app component tree
npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
dependencies: None — can be implemented independently, though P6-R2-19 (logger) enhances error logging if available
risks: ErrorBoundary does not catch errors in event handlers, async code, or server-side rendering — this is a known React limitation, not a scope gap
implementation_notes: Use a class component (React ErrorBoundary requires lifecycle methods not available in function components). Consider a simple fallback with an error message and a 'Reload' button. Place the ErrorBoundary in the component tree above the router but below any providers that should remain active during error recovery.
